title: TriageCase
description: A single de-identified ED presentation a trainee will triage. Every data source (mimic_demo, synthetic, mimic_full, mietic) normalizes to this shape. The app never reads a source-specific format.

source.description: Which loader produced this case. Used for filtering and attribution, never for branching clinical logic.

ageBand.description: De-identified age band, e.g. '25-34'. Never an exact age (HIPAA Safe Harbor).

chiefComplaint.description: Free-text chief complaint, shown to trainee at case load.

history.description: Facts the LLM patient persona may reveal when asked. The trainee must elicit these; they are NOT shown up front.

hpi.description: History of present illness narrative the persona draws from.

redFlags.description: Critical findings a competent history would surface; scored under history completeness.

expert.description: Expert/reference labels the trainee is scored against. Hidden until FEEDBACK stage.

esi.description: Reference ESI acuity level (1 = most acute). The primary scoring target.

esiRationale.description: Why this ESI level; shown in feedback.

criticalInterventions.description: Interventions an expert would initiate at triage. Scored under intervention recognition.

resourcesPredicted.description: ESI resource count (labs, imaging, etc.) used to disambiguate ESI 3 vs 4 vs 5.

outcome.description: Real downstream outcome where the source provides it (MIMIC). Optional; absent for purely synthetic cases.

diagnosisCategories.description: De-identified diagnosis groupings, never raw ICD free-text with identifiers.

provenance.description: Where this case came from and under what terms. Required for publication and attribution.

deidentified.description: Must be true for any case the app loads. The loader rejects false.

vitals.description: Triage vital signs. Null means 'not yet measured by trainee' on the live encounter; on a case it is the ground-truth value revealed when measured.
